propositions: Parallel agent sessions racing to land on FFO main force manual rebase-and-retry, causing redundant CI runs and wasted agent tokens.
A serial auto-lander lands the front Autosubmit change/chain by rebasing to tip, awaiting fresh Verified +1, and ancestor-atomic submitting the exact tested SHA.
Agents set an opt-in Autosubmit lease and run a single land command that returns one typed terminal outcome, with the bot as the single source of truth.
The stack is the atomic unit for landing and failure: never partial-land or evict within a stack; on failure the whole stack is handed back to its owning agent.
Landing logic stays platform-specific project infrastructure and adds no Gerrit-specific landing logic to rebar core.
Security invariants preserve the two-vote gate: non-gating bot-only Autosubmit label and re-verify submittability on the exact tested SHA before submit; cross-stack batching is deferred.
